title: Named Journey Metadata
description: Schema for a named journey's governance laws (e.g. Checkout Flow). A named journey owns a scope code and a set of citable laws; each law may have per-step provisions, may inherit the generic journey laws via journey.extends, and may refine (supersede) a generic journey law.

$schema.description: Path to this JSON Schema for IDE validation and autocomplete.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this journey (e.g. 'checkout-flow').

scope.description: Scope code that prefixes every law id in this file (e.g. 'CHK').

extends.description: Always ['journey'] today — opts this journey into the generic journey-tier laws. The generator unions this journey's keywords onto those laws' triggers.

description.description: What this journey represents and why it needs journey-level governance.

steps.description: Ordered step definitions, referenced by law provisions for human-readable labels.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this step (e.g. 'order-confirmation').

description.description: What happens in this step and what the user is trying to accomplish.

isFinalStep.description: True for the terminal step of the journey.

laws.description: The journey's governance laws — each a citable rule, validated holistically against the full composition of a step.

aiHints.description: Hints for AI agents consuming this journey.

keywords.description: Terms that trigger this journey's laws during intent matching. Become the laws' triggers in the generated index.

notes.description: Natural language guidance for AI agents on how to apply these laws holistically.

severity.description: How hard the law pushes back. error = hard block; warning = flagged for review; info = advisory.

lawId.description: Authored, stable law citation (e.g. 'CHK-1'). Never reused once retired.

title.description: Short human sentence naming the law.

refines.description: Id of a generic journey law this law supersedes (lex specialis). Where both apply, this one wins and the generic law is suppressed.

statement.description: The binding text of the law.

rationale.description: Why the law exists — the prose justification.

appliesWhen.description: The trigger / jurisdiction. Common keys: journey, step, scope, always.

provisions.description: The same law applied to specific steps (the decimal citation, e.g. CHK-1.3).

step.description: Id of the step this provision applies to.

labelHint.description: Suggested label text. Informational only — not enforced.

type.description: Whether the configuration must be present or must not appear.

variant.description: The specific variant this constraint targets. Omit to apply to all variants.

quantity.description: How many instances are permitted or required across the full step composition.
